wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GoNow project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a European Go player analytics platform, its key features including player search, profile visualization, favorites management, and AI-powered chat assistant. Document the technology stack (React 19, FastAPI, EGD GraphQL API, OpenRouter), architecture patterns, and how it serves both casual Go players and serious tournament participants. Include the project's goals, target audience, and unique value proposition in the Go community ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: FastAPI Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application initialization and configuration. Document the app setup, middleware configuration including CORS settings, environment variable loading with dotenv, router mounting, and health check endpoints. Explain the application lifecycle, startup procedures, and configuration options. Include details about error handling strategies, logging setup, and performance tuning parameters.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure and architecture. Document the root App component setup, routing configuration with React Router 7, QueryClient initialization with TanStack Query, and the overall application bootstrap process. Explain the component hierarchy, provider patterns, and how the application initializes its core services. Include details about the Vite build configuration, TypeScript setup, and development environment configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Architecture & Loop
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the agentic chat architecture and iterative reasoning loop. Explain the agent_chat function implementation, the conversation flow from user input to final response, and how the system handles tool calling iterations. Document the message history management, context injection, and the fallback mechanism when maximum iterations are reached. Include details about the OpenRouter API integration, error handling strategies, and the orchestration pattern that enables autonomous data lookup capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Player Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for player-related REST endpoints. Document the GET /api/search endpoint for player search by name or PIN with query parameters and response schemas. Document GET /api/player/{pin} for retrieving detailed player information including rating history. Document GET /api/player/{pin}/games for game history with pagination support (page, limit parameters). Document GET /api/player/{pin}/tournaments for tournament history. Include request/response examples, parameter validation rules, error codes (404 for not found, 500 for server errors), and data transformation details like rating history extraction from EGD data.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GoNow system overview section. Explain the full-stack architecture with React frontend, FastAPI backend, EGD GraphQL integration, and OpenRouter AI services. Document the high-level component interactions, data flows between layers, and the overall system design. Include architectural diagrams showing how frontend components communicate with backend APIs and external services. Explain the technology stack choices and their rationale.

wiki_catalogs.name: Player Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for player-related endpoints. Document the GET /api/search endpoint for player search by name or PIN with typo-tolerant matching, including query parameters and response schemas. Document GET /api/player/{pin} for retrieving complete player profiles with rating history transformation. Document GET /api/player/{pin}/games for paginated game history retrieval with page and limit parameters. Document GET /api/player/{pin}/tournaments for tournament history with date sorting. Include request/response examples, error handling (404 for not found, 500 for server errors), parameter validation, and integration with the EGD client service.

wiki_catalogs.name: EGD Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the EGD GraphQL API client implementation. Detail the EGDClient class architecture, authentication mechanism with Bearer tokens, and async HTTP communication using httpx. Document the caching strategy with TTL-based expiration, including cache key generation and memory management. Explain all GraphQL query methods: search_players for typo-tolerant name search, get_player_by_pin for detailed player profiles, get_player_games for game history, and get_player_tournaments for tournament data. Include error handling patterns, timeout configurations, and rate limiting considerations. Provide usage examples showing how to integrate the client with other services and handle response data transformations.

wiki_catalogs.name: EGD GraphQL Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the European Go Database (EGD) GraphQL integration. Document the EGDClient class implementation, including authentication with Bearer tokens, GraphQL query patterns for player search and details retrieval, and the caching mechanism with TTL-based expiration. Explain the async HTTP client usage with httpx, error handling strategies for GraphQL responses, and rate limiting considerations. Include examples of all available queries (search_players, get_player_by_pin, get_player_games, get_player_tournaments) with their parameters and response structures. Document the singleton pattern implementation and best practices for extending the client with new queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React component structure and hierarchy. Document the root App component configuration, component composition patterns, and the relationship between layout components (Navbar, ChatWidget) and page components (SearchPage, ProfilePage, FavoritesPage). Explain the component lifecycle, prop passing patterns, and how components interact with each other. Include code examples showing component usage and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: FastAPI Application Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application core. Document the application initialization process, environment variable loading with python-dotenv, CORS middleware configuration for cross-origin requests, and router mounting patterns. Explain the health check endpoint implementation, API metadata configuration (title, description, version), and the overall application bootstrap sequence. Include examples of adding new routers, configuring additional middleware, and setting up application-level dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: SearchPage Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SearchPage component. Document the search functionality including typo-tolerant matching, debounced search implementation, and player result display. Explain the use of React Query for data fetching with caching strategies, error handling patterns, and loading states. Detail the player card components, grade visualization with Go stone styling, country flag display, and favorites integration. Include examples of search query handling, navigation to player profiles, and responsive grid layout implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Loop Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the agentic chat loop implementation. Explain the iterative reasoning process, how the agent handles tool calls versus text responses, and the conversation management system. Document the message history handling, context injection, and the fallback mechanism when maximum iterations are reached. Include examples of the loop flow, error handling strategies, and performance considerations. Address the safety measures implemented to prevent infinite loops and resource exhaustion.

wiki_catalogs.name: Players Router
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Players Router module. Detail all REST endpoints including search_players, get_player, get_player_games, and get_player_tournaments. Document request/response schemas, query parameters with validation rules (min_length, ge, le constraints), path parameter handling, and error responses. Explain the intelligent search logic that prioritizes PIN lookups over name searches, rating history transformation for chart visualization, and pagination support for games endpoint. Include examples of successful responses, error scenarios, and integration patterns with the EGD client service.

wiki_catalogs.name: EGD GraphQL Client
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the EGD GraphQL client implementation. Document the EGDClient class architecture including connection management with httpx.AsyncClient, authentication via Bearer tokens, and GraphQL query execution patterns. Explain the caching mechanism with TTL-based expiration, error handling strategies for HTTP and GraphQL errors, and response parsing logic. Detail all available methods: search_players for typo-tolerant name searches, get_player_by_pin for detailed player information, get_player_games for game history retrieval, get_player_tournaments for tournament analysis, and get_player_by_name_or_pin for flexible lookups. Include examples of GraphQL query construction, variable binding, pagination handling, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started

<blog>

# GoNow: European Go Player Analytics Platform

## Project Introduction

GoNow is a full-stack web application designed to track and analyze European Go players' progress over time. The platform provides comprehensive player search capabilities, detailed profiles with rating evolution visualizations, favorites management, and an innovative AI-powered chat assistant that can autonomously query real player data through the European Go Database (EGD).

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Player Discovery**: Enable users to search European Go players by name or EGD PIN with typo-tolerant matching
- **Performance Tracking**: Visualize player rating evolution and tournament performance through interactive charts
- **AI-Powered Insights**: Provide intelligent analysis through an agentic chat system that can look up real-time player data
- **Community Engagement**: Allow users to save favorite players and track their progress over time
- **Themed Experience**: Deliver a Go-inspired user interface with authentic board game aesthetics

### Target Audience

The application serves Go enthusiasts, tournament organizers, coaches, and players who want to analyze European Go statistics, track player development, and gain insights into competitive performance trends.

## Technical Architecture

GoNow follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation of concerns between frontend and backend services, leveraging cloud-based AI capabilities for intelligent features.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[EGD GraphQL API]
    B --> D[OpenRouter AI API]
    
    subgraph "Client Layer"
        A1[React 19 + TypeScript]
        A2[Vite Build System]
        A3[React Router]
        A4[TanStack Query]
    end
    
    subgraph "Server Layer"
        B1[FastAPI Application]
        B2[REST API Routes]
        B3[Service Layer]
        B4[Data Models]
    end
    
    subgraph "External Services"
        C1[European Go Database]
        C2[OpenRouter LLM API]
    end
    
    A1 --> A2
    A2 --> A3
    A3 --> A4
    A4 --> B1
    B1 --> B2
    B2 --> B3
    B3 --> C1
    B3 --> C2
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture:**
- **React 19** with TypeScript for type-safe component development
- **Vite** as the build tool for fast development and optimized production builds
- **React Router** for client-side routing between search, profile, and favorites pages
- **TanStack Query** for server state management and caching
- **Recharts** for interactive rating evolution visualizations
- **Axios** for HTTP client communication with the backend API

**Backend Architecture:**
- **FastAPI** for high-performance async REST API endpoints
- **Pydantic** for data validation and serialization
- **httpx** for async HTTP requests to external APIs
- **In-memory caching** with configurable TTL for EGD API responses
- **CORS middleware** for cross-origin request handling

**AI Integration:**
- **OpenRouter API** for accessing multiple LLM providers
- **Native tool calling** for agentic behavior without orchestration frameworks
- **Configurable models** supporting Gemini, GPT-4o-mini, and Claude variants

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Service Layer Pattern**: Clear separation between API routes and business logic in service modules
- **Singleton Pattern**: Reusable EGD client instance with shared caching
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic tool execution based on LLM function calls
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time chat message updates with automatic scrolling
- **Strategy Pattern**: Configurable AI model selection via environment variables

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point** (`backend/app/main.py`):
- FastAPI application initialization with CORS configuration
- Router mounting for players and chat endpoints
- Health check endpoint for monitoring
- Environment variable loading from `.env` file

**Frontend Entry Point** (`frontend/src/App.tsx`):
- React application root with QueryClientProvider setup
- BrowserRouter configuration with route definitions
- Global styling and layout structure
- Chat widget integration across all pages

### Core Modules

**Player Management** (`backend/app/routers/players.py`):
- Search functionality with numeric PIN optimization
- Player detail retrieval with rating history extraction
- Tournament and game history endpoints
- Error handling and response formatting

**AI Chat System** (`backend/app/services/chat_agent.py`):
- Agentic loop implementation with tool calling
- Message history management with context preservation
- Iterative reasoning process with maximum iteration limits
- Tool result processing and final answer generation

**EGD Integration** (`backend/app/services/egd_client.py`):
- GraphQL API client with authentication
- Response caching with 5-minute TTL
- Multiple query methods for different data types
- Error handling and retry logic

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`backend/.env`):
- EGD API token for database access
- OpenRouter API key for AI functionality
- Model selection and iteration limits
- Development vs production settings

**Build Configuration** (`Makefile`):
- Cross-platform development orchestration
- Dependency installation automation
- Server lifecycle management
- Build and cleanup utilities

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/requirements.txt`):
- FastAPI >= 0.115 for async web framework
- Uvicorn >= 0.34 for ASGI server
- httpx >= 0.28 for async HTTP client
- python-dotenv >= 1.1 for environment management
- Pydantic >= 2.11 for data validation

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`):
- React 19 with TypeScript support
- Vite 8 for modern build pipeline
- React Router 7 for navigation
- TanStack Query 5 for data fetching
- Recharts 3 for charting
- Axios 1 for HTTP requests

### Integration Points

**EGD GraphQL API** (`https://europeangodatabase.eu/api/v2026.02/graphql`):
- Player search and lookup operations
- Tournament and game history retrieval
- Rating and grade information access
- Biographical data and photo URLs

**OpenRouter AI API** (`https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions`):
- Multi-model LLM access
- Native tool calling support
- Streaming response capabilities
- Cost-effective model selection

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Component] --> B[Navbar]
    A --> C[Routes]
    A --> D[ChatWidget]
    
    C --> E[SearchPage]
    C --> F[ProfilePage]
    C --> G[FavoritesPage]
    
    E --> H[PlayerSearch]
    E --> I[PlayerResults]
    
    F --> J[PlayerProfile]
    F --> K[RatingChart]
    F --> L[TournamentHistory]
    
    G --> M[FavoritesList]
    
    D --> N[ChatMessages]
    D --> O[ChatInput]
    
    H --> P[API Client]
    J --> P
    M --> P
    N --> P
    
    P --> Q[Backend API]
    Q --> R[EGD Client]
    Q --> S[Chat Agent]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Player Search and Discovery**:
- Typo-tolerant name search with fuzzy matching
- Direct PIN lookup for known players
- Pagination support for large result sets
- Country filtering and sorting options

**Detailed Player Profiles**:
- Comprehensive player information display
- Interactive rating evolution charts using Recharts
- Tournament participation history
- Game-by-game performance breakdown
- Photo and biographical information

**Favorites Management**:
- Local storage persistence for saved players
- Quick access to favorite player profiles
- Progress tracking for personal favorites
- Import/export capabilities

**AI-Powered Chat Assistant**:
- Natural language queries about players and statistics
- Autonomous data lookup through tool calling
- Comparative analysis between players
- Context-aware responses based on current page
- Real-time conversation history

### Implementation Highlights

**Agentic Chat System** (`backend/app/services/chat_agent.py`):
- Native OpenRouter tool calling implementation
- Iterative reasoning loop with maximum 3 iterations
- Automatic tool result processing and feedback
- Context preservation across conversation turns

**Real-time Data Visualization** (`frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx`):
- Interactive Recharts components for rating trends
- Responsive chart design with zoom and pan capabilities
- Color-coded performance indicators
- Export functionality for data analysis

**Go-Themed User Interface** (`frontend/src/index.css`):
- Custom CSS variables for consistent theming
- Wood texture backgrounds and stone-style badges
- Authentic Go board grid patterns
- Responsive design for mobile devices

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Searching: User Input
    Searching --> Results: Search Complete
    Results --> Profile: Select Player
    Profile --> ChartView: View Ratings
    Profile --> Favorites: Add Favorite
    Favorites --> Profile: View Favorite
    ChartView --> Profile: Back
    Profile --> Idle: Navigate Away
    Favorites --> Idle: Navigate Away
    
    state Searching {
        [*] --> Validating
        Validating --> Querying: Valid Input
        Validating --> Error: Invalid Input
        Querying --> Results: Success
        Querying --> Error: Network Error
        Error --> Searching: Retry
    }
    
    state ChatFlow {
        [*] --> MessageReceived
        MessageReceived --> ToolDecision: Analyze Request
        ToolDecision --> ExecuteTools: Call Tools
        ToolDecision --> GenerateResponse: No Tools Needed
        ExecuteTools --> ProcessResults: Get Results
        ProcessResults --> ToolDecision: More Analysis
        ProcessResults --> GenerateResponse: Final Answer
        GenerateResponse --> [*]: Send Response
    }
```

The GoNow platform represents a sophisticated integration of modern web technologies, AI capabilities, and domain-specific analytics tools, providing Go enthusiasts with powerful insights into European competitive play while maintaining an engaging and authentic user experience.
